decks.name: Vowels
decks.description: The 6 single vowels and their multiple sounds

cards.back: /a/ as in CAT 🐱, /ay/ as in CAKE 🎂, /ah/ as in DAD 👨
cards.hint: This vowel has 3 sounds — short, long, and broad

cards.back: /e/ as in BED 🛏️, /ee/ as in BE 🐝
cards.hint: The most common letter in English — 2 sounds

cards.back: /i/ as in SIT 🪑, /eye/ as in KITE 🪁
cards.hint: Short I is one of the trickiest sounds — 2 sounds

cards.back: /o/ as in DOG 🐶, /oh/ as in GO 🏃, /oo/ as in DO ✅
cards.hint: The oldest letter — 3 sounds

cards.back: /u/ as in CUP ☕, /yoo/ as in USE 🔧
cards.hint: U always follows Q — 2 sounds

cards.back: /y/ as in YES ✅, /ee/ as in HAPPY 😊, /eye/ as in FLY ✈️, /i/ as in GYM 🏋️
cards.hint: Both a consonant AND a vowel — 4 sounds

decks.name: Consonants
decks.description: The 20 single consonants and their sounds

cards.hint: Silent in LAMB and CLIMB

cards.back: /k/ as in CAT 🐱, CUP ☕ | /s/ as in ICE 🧊, CITY 🏙️
cards.hint: C says /s/ before E, I, Y — zero exceptions (Rule 1)
One of the most reliable consonants
Used to be written as FF in Old English

cards.back: /g/ as in GO 🏃, BIG 🐘 | /j/ as in GEM 💎, GIANT 🦕
cards.hint: G TENDS to soften before E, I, Y — but has exceptions (Rule 2)
Silent in HOUR, HONEST, and HEIR
The last letter added to the English alphabet
Silent before N as in KNEE and KNOW
Sometimes silent as in WALK, TALK, and CALM
One of the first sounds babies learn to make
The 6th most common letter in English
Silent in PSALM, PNEUMONIA, and PSYCHOLOGY
Q never goes anywhere without U — always QU
R controls vowels when it follows them

cards.back: /s/ as in SUN ☀️, SIT 🪑 | /z/ as in IS, HIS, DOGS
cards.hint: S says /z/ between vowels and after voiced consonants
One of the most common consonants
English words never end in V alone — needs E
W warps the A sound — WATCH sounds like WOTCH

cards.back: /ks/ as in BOX 📦, FOX 🦊 | /z/ as in XYLOPHONE 🎵
cards.hint: Says /z/ at the start of words, /ks/ elsewhere
Z is one of the least used letters in English

decks.name: Consonant Teams
decks.description: 14 consonant teams — two or three letters making one sound

cards.hint: One of the most reliable consonant teams

cards.back: /th/ voiced as in THIS, THAT | /th/ unvoiced as in THINK, MATH
cards.hint: Two sounds — voiced (vibrates) and unvoiced (breathy)

cards.back: /ch/ as in CHIP 🍟, CHAIN ⛓️ | /k/ as in SCHOOL 🏫 | /sh/ as in CHEF 👨‍🍳
cards.hint: Three sounds — English /ch/, Greek /k/, French /sh/
Some dialects pronounce the H, others just say /w/
Greek spelling for the /f/ sound
The bodyguard — used right after a single short vowel
The N becomes /ng/ before K
A nasal sound made at the back of the throat

cards.back: /g/ as in GHOST 👻 | silent as in NIGHT 🌙
cards.hint: Says /g/ at the start, silent in the middle/end
K is always silent before N
W is always silent before R
G is silent in GN
The D shields the short vowel before the /j/ sound
The T shields the short vowel before the /ch/ sound

decks.name: Vowel Teams
decks.description: 18 vowel teams — two vowels working together

cards.hint: Always says /ee/ — one of the most reliable vowel teams

cards.back: /ee/ as in BEAN 🫘, READ 📖 | /e/ as in BREAD 🍞 | /ay/ as in BREAK 💔
cards.hint: Three sounds — /ee/ is most common
Always says long O — very reliable
Used in the MIDDLE of words (Rule 10)
Used at the END of words (Rule 10)

cards.back: /ow/ as in OUT 👉, /oo/ as in YOU, /u/ as in TOUCH 🤲
cards.hint: Multiple sounds — /ow/ is most common

cards.back: /ow/ as in COW 🐄, OWL 🦉 | /oh/ as in SNOW ❄️, GROW 🌱
cards.hint: Two sounds — /ow/ and /oh/
Used in the MIDDLE of words
Used at the END of words — partner of AU

cards.back: /oo/ as in NEW 🆕, BLEW 💨 | /yoo/ as in FEW 🤏, DEW 💧
cards.hint: Two sounds — /oo/ and /yoo/

cards.back: /oo/ as in MOON 🌙, FOOD 🍕 | /oo/ short as in BOOK 📚, COOK 👨‍🍳
cards.hint: Two sounds — long /oo/ and short /oo/
Used in the MIDDLE of words (Rule 9)
Used at the END of words (Rule 9)

cards.back: /ay/ as in VEIN 💉, REIN 🐴 | /ee/ as in CEILING 🏠
cards.hint: Two sounds — I before E except after C

cards.back: /ay/ as in THEY 👥, GREY 🩶 | /ee/ as in KEY 🔑, MONEY 💰
cards.hint: Two sounds — /ay/ and /ee/

cards.back: /ee/ as in FIELD 🏟️, THIEF 🦹 | /eye/ as in PIE 🥧, TIE 👔
cards.hint: Two sounds — /ee/ and /eye/

cards.back: /oo/ as in BLUE 🔵, TRUE ✅ | /yoo/ as in CUE 🎱, RESCUE 🚑
cards.hint: E guards U — English words don't end in U alone

cards.back: /oo/ as in FRUIT 🍎, JUICE 🧃 | /i/ as in BUILD 🏗️
cards.hint: Two sounds — /oo/ is more common

decks.name: R-Controlled Vowels
decks.description: 5 R-controlled vowels where R changes the vowel sound

cards.hint: R takes control — makes A say /ar/
One of the three /er/ triplets — ER, IR, UR all sound the same
Sounds just like ER and UR — a spelling choice
The third /er/ triplet — same sound as ER and IR
R controls the O sound
GH is silent — three letters for one sound
GH is silent — four letters for /ay/

cards.back: /oh/ as in THOUGH, /oo/ as in THROUGH, /ow/ as in PLOUGH, /uf/ as in ROUGH, /off/ as in COUGH, /aw/ as in THOUGHT
cards.hint: The trickiest phonogram — SIX different sounds!
Always says /aw/ — four letters, one sound

decks.name: Special Phonograms
decks.description: 6 special phonograms used in suffixes and borrowed words

cards.hint: Says /sh/ at the start of a suffix — Latin origin
Says /sh/ at the start of a suffix — Latin origin

cards.back: /sh/ as in SESSION 📋, TENSION ⚡ | /zh/ as in VISION 👁️, DIVISION ➗
cards.hint: Two sounds — /sh/ and /zh/ (voiced version)
Quite rare — /sh/ from Latin roots

cards.back: /ed/ as in WANTED 🙏, /d/ as in CALLED 📞, /t/ as in JUMPED 🦘
cards.hint: Three sounds — depends on the last sound of the base word
Q and U are inseparable — Q never appears without U
